R0165.title: IoT Botnets
R0165.definition: Large numbers of compromised IoT devices organized into botnets for launching distributed attacks or other malicious activities.
R0165.description: Attackers exploit security vulnerabilities in IoT devices (such as default passwords, unpatched vulnerabilities) to control devices in bulk, forming botnets (such as Mirai, Mozi). These botnets can launch large-scale DDoS attacks, perform cryptocurrency mining, send spam, or serve as attack springboards.
R0165.influence: Device performance degradation or failure, becoming part of attack infrastructure, affecting internet stability.

references.title: [PDF] Analyzing The Mirai IoT Botnet and Its Recent Variants - arXiv
